concept: Successful GAN training relies on a checklist of practical tricks developed by researchers to stabilize the competitive learning dynamics.
title: A Practitioner's Toolbox

exercises.explain: In GANs, both the Generator and Discriminator must learn at similar rates so they can challenge each other effectively without one totally overpowering the other.
exercises.prompt: What is one of the biggest challenges in training a stable Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)?

options.text: Maintaining a dynamic balance between Generator and Discriminator
Training only the Generator and leaving the Discriminator frozen
Making the Discriminator 100 times faster than the Generator

cards.body: Training a GAN is notorious for being unstable. Without special tweaks, the Generator might fail completely or get stuck producing the exact same image over and over. That's why practitioners rely on a proven 'toolbox' of training tricks!
cards.title: Why GANs Need a Toolbox

cards.body: The GAN Practitioner's Toolbox is a collection of practical heuristics—such as using leaky activation functions, adjusting label values, and adding noise—specifically designed to keep the training process stable and balanced.
cards.title: The Practitioner's Toolbox

cards.body: Think of training a GAN like two chefs competing to make the best soup. If one chef gets way better too quickly, the other gets discouraged and quits. The practitioner is the head chef, constantly tweaking the kitchen rules and ingredients to keep the competition close and exciting.
cards.title: Balancing the Kitchen

cards.body: Instead of telling the Discriminator that real images are exactly '1' and fakes are exactly '0', we use 'One-Sided Label Smoothing'. We might label real images as '0.9'. This small change prevents the Discriminator from becoming too confident and overpowering the Generator.
cards.title: Label Smoothing in Action
The ReLU Trap
cards.body: You might think that using the standard ReLU function is always best because it works elsewhere. Actually, standard ReLUs can 'die' and block gradients. Practitioners use 'Leaky ReLUs' instead, allowing a small flow of information even for negative values.

left.text: Blocks all negative values completely, which can freeze learning in the Generator.

right.text: Allows a small leak of negative values to keep gradients flowing and learning active.

exercises.explain: Label smoothing prevents the Discriminator from becoming too extreme or overconfident, keeping the competition healthy.
exercises.prompt: How does 'One-Sided Label Smoothing' help during GAN training?

options.text: It makes all fake images look blurry on purpose.
It reduces overconfidence in the Discriminator by using targets like 0.9 instead of 1.0.
It trains the Generator without using any real data.

exercises.explain: Leaky ReLUs allow a tiny gradient for negative values, preventing neurons from 'dying' and freezing the training process.
exercises.prompt: True or False: Using Leaky ReLU instead of standard ReLU is a common trick to prevent dead gradients in GANs.
To prevent the Discriminator from becoming overly confident, practitioners apply label __________ during training.
exercises.sentence: To prevent the Discriminator from becoming overly confident, practitioners apply label smoothing during training.
exercises.explain: Label smoothing softens target values (like using 0.9 instead of 1) to keep the Discriminator from getting too dominant.

options.text: A high-resolution, diverse dataset of distinct cat breeds.
A generator that produces the exact same distorted face with identical patterns every time.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario describes a GAN that is failing due to lack of stabilization tricks?
exercises.explain: When a GAN lacks training tricks like Leaky ReLUs and proper scaling, it often fails to learn diverse patterns, resulting in repetitive, corrupted outputs.

options.text: Apply tricks like Leaky ReLU and label smoothing to keep gradients flowing and balance competition.
Stop training the generator and only train the discriminator on real images.
Avoid using any activation functions in the neural networks.

exercises.prompt: If your GAN is suffering from unstable training and dead gradients, what should you do?
exercises.explain: This lesson on practitioner tricks concludes our toolbox section. Next, we will explore advanced evaluation metrics for GANs!
